title: fix(image): surface real fetch error when user image URL fails
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- `downloadUserImage` failures (user-provided `image=` URL returns 4xx, DNS fails, etc.) were surfacing as the generic `Image provider error` with body `{}` — actual cause hidden from the caller
- Root: `HttpError(..., 400)` had no `details`, so the classifier treated it as a non-validation 4xx and stripped the message
- Fix: include the failing URL in the message and mark `details: { validation: true }` so the classifier preserves it as `Invalid image request: Failed to fetch image <URL>: 503 Service Unavailable`
- Affects every model that takes a user image (kontext, gptimage, seedream, klein, nanobanana, all i2v video models)

## Test plan
- [x] `npx vitest run test/video-error-classification.test.ts test/image/seedreamModel.test.ts` → 32/32 pass
- [ ] Manual: hit `/image/x?model=ltx-2&image=<broken-url>` and confirm the response body contains the real upstream status + URL

gist.headline: No more mystery errors
gist.blurb: When an image URL you provide fails to load, we now tell you exactly why instead of throwing a generic error. Debugging your prompts just got a whole lot easier!
gist.summary: Image generation requests that fail to download a user-provided image URL now return the specific upstream HTTP status and URL instead of a generic 'Image provider error'.
gist.impact: Developers can now easily debug broken image inputs across all image-to-image and image-to-video models without guessing why the fetch failed.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee acting as a detective, using a magnifying glass to inspect a broken picture frame or a piece of paper with a URL on it, discovering the real error code.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee wearing a tiny detective hat, using a magnifying glass to inspect a broken picture frame with a '404' error code inside. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
